evidence: handleFullDependencySketch in SketchHandlers is a single-line pass-through: `handleDepSketchForType(exchange, lemma)` with no added logic; its Javadoc restates what the delegate does

CollocateProfileComparator.compareCollocateProfiles logs both input parameters at entry: `logger.debug("Nouns: {}", seedNouns)` and `logger.debug("Min logDice: {}", minLogDice)` — input-echo logging at entry

CollocateProfileComparator.buildRawAdjectiveProfiles emits three debug lines per noun inside the loop: entry log ("\nProfiling: {}"), count log ("Found {} adjectives"), and top-5 dump (`logger.debug("  Top 5: {}", adjectives.subList(...))`)
GrammarConfigLoader.missingConfigException(Path p) is a single-line private factory used exactly once in readConfigFile — over-extracted for a trivial message

strengths: Domain vocabulary is precise throughout: `exploreByPattern`, `buildCollocateToNounsMap`, `filterByMinShared`, `identifyCoreCollocates` — no handleData/processItem/doOperation generics
Comments explain WHY rather than restating code: constructor I/O note in WordSketchApiServer, rotation strategy in ConlluConverter, thread-interrupt handling in Main all add genuine context
`wrapWithErrorHandling` genuinely adds error-mapping logic (RequestEntityTooLargeException → 413, IAE → 400, IOException → 500) — not a pass-through
Debug logging is guarded appropriately: `logTopProfiles` checks `logger.isDebugEnabled()` before expensive operations
Wrapper methods that bind a constant argument (handleSurfaceRelations → handleRelationsForType(exchange, RelationType.SURFACE)) serve as named route-registration points, not dead indirection
ai_generated_debt.issue_character: Issues are isolated and mild — one clear pass-through private method and per-iteration input-echo debug logging in the profiler loop, not a systemic pattern across the codebase.
ai_generated_debt.score_rationale: The codebase avoids the worst AI-debt markers: domain vocabulary is precise, comments add insight, and wrappers generally add logic. The per-noun debug loop in CollocateProfileComparator (three logger.debug calls per iteration) and the one-line pass-through `handleFullDependencySketch` are genuine AI-hallmark patterns but limited in scope. This places the code in the solid-but-uneven range (75–82), closer to the upper end because the issues are few and localized rather than systemic.

issues.summary: handleFullDependencySketch is a pure single-line delegation with a restating docstring
evidence: Method body: `handleDepSketchForType(exchange, lemma);` — no added logic

Javadoc says 'Handle full dependency sketch request. Returns all dependency relations for the given lemma. DEP relations use surface patterns with [deprel=...] constraints.' — this restates what handleDepSketchForType does
issues.suggestion: Inline the call into handleSketchRequest's dispatch block where it is the only caller, or collapse handleDepSketchForType into this method if the naming distinction adds no value

issues.summary: CollocateProfileComparator.buildRawAdjectiveProfiles emits three debug lines per noun in a hot loop
evidence: `logger.debug("  Found {} adjectives", adjectives.size())` on every noun
`logger.debug("  Top 5: {}", adjectives.subList(0, Math.min(5, adjectives.size())))` on every noun — dumps a list object per iteration
issues.suggestion: Collect a per-noun summary in a local list and emit a single `logger.debug("Profiled {} nouns: {}", count, summary)` after the loop, matching the style of SemanticFieldExplorer which logs entry and completion summaries rather than per-item entries

root_causes: Incremental extraction of single-use private helpers without checking whether the caller complexity justifies the extraction
